findings.detail: All imports from stdlib only (hashlib, json, sqlite3, datetime, pathlib). No imports from marketmind modules — zero back-import risk.
Single public class LearningStore with 4-table schema (predictions, lessons, entity_memories, calibration_data). Context manager support (__enter__/__exit__). Clean lifecycle management via close().

Predictions: PredictableHypothesis objects → INSERT OR REPLACE → dict on SELECT. Lessons: dict → INSERT OR REPLACE → list[dict] on SELECT. Entity memories: entity_id + dict → INSERT OR REPLACE. Calibration: tracker_id + dict → INSERT OR REPLACE. Consistent pattern — all writes are idempotent (INSERT OR REPLACE).
Standalone module — no existing callers. Designed to be consumed by prediction_extractor output and calibration_tracker. Schema uses IF NOT EXISTS for all CREATE TABLE statements.

Four tables with appropriate indexes: predictions (status, expiry_date, entity), lessons (entity, root_cause), entity_memories (entity_type), calibration_data (no secondary index — tracker_id is PK). Indexes support the query patterns in the module methods.

notes: Clean storage module with consistent CRUD patterns. All writes use INSERT OR REPLACE for idempotency. Schema initialization is safe (IF NOT EXISTS). The module is designed as a leaf node in the dependency DAG — it imports nothing from marketmind and is imported by calibration_tracker and pipeline orchestration.
